message:
fix(cli,db): eliminate stale WAL/SHM sidecar propagation and add sqlite3 CLI health probe

The root cause of recurring "malformed database image" failures during
backup/restore/migration was stale WAL and SHM sidecar files being
blindly copied alongside the main database file. When a WAL checkpoint
has already folded pending writes into the main DB, the leftover sidecar
references pages that no longer exist, causing SQLite to read garbage.

Changes to backup/restore (cli/lib.rs):
- create_db_backup() now runs PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before
  copying, ensuring the main DB file is self-contained, then no longer
  copies WAL/SHM sidecars at all.
- restore_db_from_backup() no longer copies WAL/SHM sidecars from the
  backup artifact. Instead it explicitly removes any leftover sidecars
  at the destination so SQLite recreates them fresh on first open.
- restore_db_from_backup() now guards against restoring to a path that
  does not yet exist (skips the pre-rollback safety copy).
- restore_db_from_backup() runs sqlite_file_is_healthy() after copying
  to fail fast if the restored DB is actually corrupt.
- New helper sqlite_sidecar_path() constructs "-wal"/"-shm" paths via
  OsString::push to avoid format!() Display coercion issues on
  non-UTF-8 paths.
- New helper sqlite_checkpoint_truncate() encapsulates the checkpoint
  pattern used by both backup and restore paths.

Changes to migration (migrate.rs):
- copy_python_database_to_rust() now checkpoints the source DB before
  copying and intentionally omits WAL/SHM sidecar copying.
- If stale destination sidecars already exist, they are removed.

New sqlite3 CLI probe (cli/lib.rs):
- sqlite_quick_check_via_cli() shells out to the system sqlite3 binary
  to run PRAGMA quick_check, catching corruption that the in-process
  libsqlite3 probe might miss (e.g., page-level inconsistencies visible
  only to a fresh connection with no cached state).
- Returns Option<bool>: None if sqlite3 is not installed, Some(ok)
  otherwise. Integrated into sqlite_doctor_sanity so the doctor command
  reports the additional probe result.

Backup candidate discovery (cli/lib.rs):
- sqlite_backup_candidates() and list_db_backups() now handle empty
  parent paths by falling back to Path::new(".") instead of returning
  early, fixing edge cases where a bare filename like "storage.sqlite3"
  was passed without any directory component.

Tests updated throughout to use real SQLite databases with marker tables
instead of raw byte slices, ensuring backup/restore round-trips exercise
actual SQLite I/O and sidecar behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
